route.name: Oregon Outback
route.region: Klamath Falls to Deschutes / Columbia River
route.description: Official/public GPX high-desert traverse on gravel, dirt roads, rail trail, and remote connectors.
route.surfaceMix: Official/public GPX high-desert traverse on gravel, dirt roads, rail trail, and remote connectors.
route.bestSeason: Spring to early summer or fall; July heat and water scarcity are serious blockers.

difficultyProfile.surface: Official/public GPX high-desert traverse on gravel, dirt roads, rail trail, and remote connectors.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Major risk: 50+ mile dry sections and roughly 80-mile reliable-water gap per route notes.
resources.food: Sparse towns and uncertain hours; plan every resupply.
resources.camping: BLM/NF/campgrounds by segment; full-route camping sequence exceeds July 10-13.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Do not promote the full route for next weekend.
Only a selected closure-free short segment should be considered.
Verify water and heat before any July segment.
hazards: forest closure/reroute north of Fort Rock

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Forest closure north of Fort Rock requires an alternate with unverified roads.
July high-desert heat and 50-80 mile water gaps are major risks.
requiredFixes: Select a short closure-free segment before further promotion.
Check OC&E Woods Line closure, fire restrictions, campsite inventory, water, heat, and Amtrak bike space.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: What changed in the July 9-10 final refresh for closures, weather, smoke, water, and camps?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Full Oregon Outback is not a July 10-13 weekend route; only a separate short segment should be considered.
